claims.evidence_text: Optimum sowing depth is around 4 cm but depends on pest threats (e.g. slugs and rooks).
claims.claim_summary: Optimum wheat sowing depth is around 4 cm.

claims.evidence_text: While sowing to emergence takes longer in cold than in warm weather, the thermal time in each case is similar (150ºC days).
claims.claim_summary: Wheat requires about 150 °C days of thermal time from sowing to emergence.

claims.evidence_text: Benchmarks: sowing to emergence 150 ºC days 11 days in September 15 days in October 26 days in November
claims.claim_summary: Wheat sowing to emergence takes ~11 days (September) to ~26 days (November).
Benchmark wheat establishment is 70% of seeds sown.

claims.evidence_text: the target should be about 150 plants/m 2 in the thinnest areas of the field and more if it is necessary to compete against weeds.
claims.claim_summary: Target wheat plant population is about 150 plants/m2 in thinnest field areas.

claims.evidence_text: average establishment for sandy soils is 90%, compared with 65% for loams and clays
claims.claim_summary: Wheat establishment averages 90% on sandy soils vs 65% on loams and clays.
